title: Group Sales & Operations Executive ( Administration & Co-Ordination)
description:
Job Summary

The Group Administrator supports the Sales and Operations teams by coordinating group bookings, preparing sales documentation, and handling essential administrative tasks. This role acts as a key liaison between sales, front office, and other operational departments to ensure accurate documentation, smooth communication, and efficient handling of group and corporate business.

Key Responsibilities

Sales Coordination & Group Support

  • Assist Sales Managers with group and corporate bookings from initial inquiry to confirmation
  • Prepare and issue contracts, proposals, and confirmation letters for groups, events, and corporate clients.
  • Coordinate rooming lists, billing instructions, special requests, and amendments for group reservations.
  • Maintain accurate sales files, contracts, and client records (physical and digital.
  • Track contract deadlines, deposits, and cut-off dates, following up as required.
  • Support sales reporting, forecasts, and internal documentation


Front Office Administrative Support

  • Assist Front Office with basic administrative paperwork related to group arrivals and departures
  • Prepare welcome letters, group resumes, rooming lists, and internal memos
  • Coordinate with Front Office, Housekeeping, Finance, and F&B to ensure group requirements are communicated clearly
  • Support billing preparation, invoicing, and filing in coordination with Finance
  • Handle guest correspondence related to group bookings and administrative inquiries


General Administrative Duties

  • Manage filing systems, databases, and shared folders
  • Answer phone calls and emails related to group and corporate inquiries
  • Prepare reports, spreadsheets, and presentations as required
  • Maintain confidentiality of guest, client, and company information
  • Provide general administrative support to the Sales and Front Office teams


Job Requirements:

  • Diploma or Degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or related field preferred
  • Previous experience in hotel sales coordination, reservations, or front office administration is an advantage
  • Strong organizational and time-management skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Attention to detail and ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint
  • Familiarity with hotel PMS and sales systems is an advantage
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team
  • Professional appearance and customer-focused attitude
  • Office-based with regular interaction with Front Office and Sales teams
  • May require flexibility during peak periods, group arrivals, or events
